meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap by building a SaaS pricing engine. Master intermediate development with this guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis ends when you shift focus from 'what to build' to 'how to monetize.' By architecting a dynamic SaaS pricing engine, you solve a universal business problem while demonstrating high-value technical skills that employers crave. It’s the perfect intermediate project to launch your portfolio today.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer's greatest adversary. You’ve mastered the syntax, you’ve completed the tutorials, yet you sit paralyzed, wondering what to build next. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of a clear, high-stakes objective. Every successful SaaS company started with a single, complex problem: how to capture value.

Today, we stop building 'to-do lists' and start building infrastructure. By developing a dynamic SaaS pricing module, you aren't just coding a feature—you are learning the logic of subscription tiers, stripe integration, and usage-based billing. This is the bridge between a student's script and a professional's product.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Before writing logic, define your data structure for Basic, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. This forces you to think about database schema design early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including price, currency, feature list, and billing interval.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file pricing_schema.ts --prompt 'Generate a robust schema for tiered SaaS subscriptions'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Create the calculation engine that converts usage metrics into billing amounts. This is the core 'business logic' layer of your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function that calculates prorated subscription costs based on the date of upgrade.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file pricing_engine.ts --instruction 'Implement prorated billing logic for monthly plans'

steps.title: Build the Stripe Integration Layer
steps.description: Connect your logic to a real-world payment gateway. This is a critical skill for any developer entering the SaaS market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for Stripe Checkout session creation using the latest Node.js SDK.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --command 'npx stripe-cli listen' --context 'Integrate Stripe SDK into current project'

steps.title: Develop the React Pricing Table
steps.description: Translate your backend logic into a high-converting UI. Focus on clear calls-to-action and feature comparison tables.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component using Tailwind CSS that displays three pricing tiers with a toggle for annual savings.

steps.title: Security and Validation
steps.description: Ensure that users cannot manipulate pricing parameters on the client side. Always validate pricing on your server.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Zod validation schemas for incoming subscription update requests to prevent price tampering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file validation.ts --add 'Zod security schemas for pricing inputs'

pro_tips: Always store prices in cents as integers to avoid floating-point math errors in production.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your backend to handle failed credit card charges without immediate service termination.
Use Feature Flags to test different pricing tiers for specific user segments without deploying new code.
Always log billing events to a secondary analytics provider; never rely solely on Stripe's dashboard for your own growth metrics.
Build a 'hidden' admin override that allows you to grant custom discounts to early beta testers.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is functional, deploy it to a staging environment and run a 'smoke test' using Stripe's test mode keys. After that, integrate it into a full-stack SaaS boilerplate.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a blank IDE into a revenue-generating machine. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the infrastructure that makes ideas possible. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
